subject: Project Management
title: Agile in Government
description: A quick, practical guide to Agile in Government.

hook.text: In 2011, the UK created the Government Digital Service to save public sector IT from failure.

buildup.text: They replaced massive contracts with small Agile teams that delivered working services fast.

discovery.text: GOV.UK launched as a simple, user-centered website — replacing thousands of confusing pages.

twist.text: The U.S. followed suit after HealthCare.gov's disaster, creating the U.S. Digital Service.

climax.text: Governments proved that Agile works even in bureaucracies — when leadership commits to it.

punchline.text: If government can go Agile, anyone can.

quiz.question: What prompted the UK to create the Government Digital Service?
options: To save public sector IT from repeated failures
To compete with private tech companies
To reduce the number of government employees
